records.ety: [F. sire, originally, an older person. See Sir.]

defs: A lord, master, or other person in authority. See Sir.
quotes: Pain and distress, sickness and ire, And melancholy that angry sire, Be of her palace senators.

defs: A tittle of respect formerly used in speaking to elders and superiors, but now only in addressing a sovereign.
A father; the head of a family; the husband.
quotes: Jankin thet was our sire [i.e., husband].
And raise his issue, like a loving sire.

defs: A creator; a maker; an author; an originator.
quotes: [He] was the sire of an immortal strain.

defs: The male parent of a beast; -- applied especially to horses; as, the horse had a good sire.
notes: ☞ Sire is often used in composition; as in grandsire, grandfather; great-grandsire, great-grandfather.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sired (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Siring.]

defs: To beget; to procreate; -- used of beasts, and especially of stallions.
